title: paspalum scrobiculatum var. auriculatum
intro: Paspalum scrobiculatum var. auriculatum is a type of grass commonly known as 'kodri' in Ayurvedic medicine. It is used to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or powder, and it is also used in the form of herbal tea or paste to treat various health issues.
